coverage_window.description: Public market-driver research from yesterday to now; UTC date 2026-06-05.

executive_driver_summary.market_sentiment: Neutral to risk-off; strong labor data is being treated as hawkish Fed repricing rather than pure growth relief.
executive_driver_summary.primary_driver: US payroll upside surprise repricing Fed expectations higher, lifting yields and USD and pressuring high-duration tech/gold.
executive_driver_summary.what_changed_from_yesterday: June 4 relief from lower oil/yields and broadening leadership shifted to June 5 Nasdaq-led weakness after NFP +172k and yield jump.
executive_driver_summary.largest_trap: Assuming strong jobs are automatically bullish for equities; today the market is pricing tighter financial conditions.
executive_driver_summary.hrsb_relevance: Relevant to VIX curve, SPX levels, rates, USD, credit and event-gap inputs; no private portfolio data used.

market_lens.market_view: Good macro news is being interpreted through Fed reaction-function risk; yields and USD are the main transmission channels.
market_lens.change_from_previous_session: From oil/yield relief and Dow/small-cap strength to NFP-driven yield shock and Nasdaq underperformance.

five_watch_points.meaning: prior close/open/psychological support and short-option risk

causal_chain: Fed cuts removed / hike risk repriced
SPX/Nasdaq and VIX risk become positioning-sensitive

confirmed_facts.fact: BLS reported May payrolls +172k, unemployment 4.3%, AHE +0.3% m/m and +3.4% y/y, prior two months +93k revisions.
AP reported S&P -1.6%, Nasdaq -2.6%, Dow -0.7% at 12:11 ET and 2Y/10Y yield jumps after payrolls.
AP reported June 4 S&P +0.4%, Dow +1.7% record, Nasdaq -0.1%, Russell +1.4% amid lower oil/yields.

interpretations: Primary driver is Fed/rates repricing, not general news flow.
Nasdaq underperformance suggests concentration/duration shock rather than confirmed credit stress.
Oil decline partially offsets inflation risk but does not override labor/wage signal.

risk_scenarios.condition: 10Y below 4.50% and Nasdaq recovers opening loss
10Y above 4.60%, USDJPY holds above 160, Nasdaq breaks lows
risk_scenarios.reaction: SPX downside and VIX futures rise

hiro_attention_points: Do not assume strong jobs are equity bullish.
Treat dealer/CTA/gamma language only as market-structure risk unless directly sourced.

commodities.move: -7.974mn bbl in week ending May 29 per EIA recap

crypto.move: not a primary HRSB driver today

central_bank_policy_expectations.detail: AP reported market saw >60% chance of a Fed hike by year-end and little/no cut chance after jobs report.

critical_information: June 16-17 FOMC is the next major policy window.
June 10 10-year auction is a rates-volatility checkpoint.
Middle East/oil headlines remain weekend gap risk.
source_quality_notes: Payrolls and Treasury supply are high-quality primary sources.
Spot VIX/VVIX/gamma/CTA thresholds are incomplete; flow comments are scenario risk only.

headline_vs_actual_driver.headline: Strong jobs
headline_vs_actual_driver.evidence: NFP +172k; 2Y/10Y yields rose; AP reported >60% year-end hike chance

headline_vs_actual_driver.headline: AI stocks sold off
headline_vs_actual_driver.actual_driver: Rate-duration and concentration risk in high valuation leadership
headline_vs_actual_driver.evidence: Nasdaq underperformed; Nvidia/Broadcom fell; breadth not uniformly weak per AP

headline_vs_actual_driver.headline: Oil down
headline_vs_actual_driver.actual_driver: Inflation relief is partial and offset by labor/wage strength
headline_vs_actual_driver.evidence: Brent $93.50 but above pre-war ~$70; AHE +3.4% y/y

cross_asset_confirmation.direction: gold down, Brent down but high
cross_asset_confirmation.notes: gold confirms rates/USD; oil partly offsets

flow_risk_map.treasury_auction_bond_supply: Medium-high: June 10Y $39bn/20Y $13bn/30Y $22bn plus Jun 10 10Y auction can pressure yields if demand is weak.
flow_risk_map.options_gamma_0dte_opex: Medium: SPX 7500 and Nasdaq weakness are positioning-sensitive; actual dealer hedging not asserted.
flow_risk_map.macro_data_fed_repricing: High: payroll surprise is current dominant driver before Jun 16-17 FOMC.
flow_risk_map.fx_intervention_carry_unwind: Medium: USDJPY near/above 160 creates intervention/carry unwind risk; no intervention claimed.
flow_risk_map.commodity_shock: Medium: Brent lower but still elevated; Middle East weekend gap risk remains.
flow_risk_map.earnings_ai_crowding: Medium-high: AI leadership vulnerable to rates and valuation reset.
flow_risk_map.month_end_index_rebalance: Low: not today's main driver; watch later June.

trap_risks: Strong jobs treated as bullish when market is focused on Fed tightening.
Low VIX interpreted as safety despite event/rates risk.
Oil decline interpreted as full inflation relief.
Assuming actual dealer/CTA activity without direct evidence.

watch_levels.basis: AP post-NFP level and psychological break

next_24_72h_scenarios.trigger: 10Y above 4.60%, USDJPY above 160, Nasdaq lower, oil/geopolitical shock
next_24_72h_scenarios.market_reaction: SPX 7500 break risk, VIX futures up, credit watch
next_24_72h_scenarios.hrsb_relevance: possible defend-risk input, final decision downstream

vix_portfolio_impact_channels: SPX level breaks can lift short-dated implied volatility.
Rates shocks can pressure high-duration equities and raise hedge demand.
Weekend geopolitical oil gaps can steepen event-vol demand.
spx_vix_rates_credit_links: If credit ETFs/spreads widen, move shifts from valuation shock to stress regime; not confirmed yet.

data_sources.title: Stocks slump as Big Tech sinks and a strong May jobs report boosts odds for higher interest rates
How major US stock indexes fared Thursday 6/4/2026
Dollar firms after strong US jobs data, pushes yen through 160 level
Gold falls over 2% as robust US jobs data cements bets on higher rates
European shares slip as Mideast tensions linger; tech stocks pause rally
Stocks drop as AI rally pauses, US-Iran peace talks stall

data_gaps: Real-time spot VIX/VVIX and SPX gamma not fully verified.
Credit spreads/HYG/LQD real-time confirmation unavailable in sourced set.
BTC not a major driver and not deeply sourced.
No direct evidence of actual dealer/CTA/macro fund trading; described only as structural risk.
